back_translation: Sarai, Abram's wife, had not borne him children. She had an Egyptian maidservant whose name was Hagar.
Sarai said to Abram, "Look, the LORD has prevented me from bearing children. Please go in to my maidservant; perhaps I can build a family through her." Abram listened to Sarai's voice.
After Abram had lived in the land of Canaan for 10 years, Sarai his wife took her Egyptian maidservant Hagar and gave her to Abram her husband as a wife.
He went in to Hagar, and she conceived. When Hagar saw that she was pregnant, she began to despise her mistress.
Sarai said to Abram, "May the wrong done to me fall upon you. I gave my maidservant into your arms, and when she saw that she was pregnant, she despised me. May the LORD judge between you and me."
Abram replied to Sarai, "Look, your maidservant is in your hands. Do with her as you see fit." Sarai oppressed Hagar, until Hagar fled from her presence.
The angel of the LORD found Hagar at a spring of water in the wilderness, at the spring on the way to Shur.
The angel said, "Hagar, servant of Sarai, where have you come from, and where are you going?" She replied, "I am fleeing from Sarai my mistress."
The angel of the LORD said to her, "Return to your mistress and submit under her authority."
The angel of the LORD also said to her, "I will greatly multiply your offspring, so that they will be too numerous to count."
The angel of the LORD continued, "Behold, you have conceived and will bear a son. You shall name him Ishmael, because the LORD has heard your cry of affliction.
He will be a wild man, like a wild donkey. His hand will be against everyone, and everyone's hand against him; he will dwell apart from all his brothers."
Hagar called the name of the LORD who spoke to her, "You are El-Roi (the God who sees me)," for she said, "Here I have seen the One who sees me."
Therefore the well was called Beer-lahai-roi (the well of the Living-One who sees me). It is between Kadesh and Bered.
Hagar bore Abram a son, and Abram named the son she bore Ishmael.
Abram was 86 years old when Hagar bore Ishmael to him.
